loading.doc: Generic loading placeholder used in lists / panes that haven't received data yet.

busy.doc: Generic in-flight status text shown while a skill call is running.

error.doc: Generic error prefix shown before any API error message, e.g. 'Error: invalid-mnemonic'.

hero_title.doc: Hero heading on /welcome.html — first thing a brand-new user sees.

hero_intro.text: A neighbourhood pinboard that runs locally on your device. No feed, no ads, your data stays yours.

new_title.doc: Title of the left-hand choice card on /welcome.html — link to /onboard.html.

new_body.text: I have an invite link or code and want to join a group for the first time.

restore_title.doc: Title of the right-hand choice card on /welcome.html — link to /restore.html.

restore_body.text: I have my recovery phrase (12 or 24 words) from another device. I want to use it here.

footer_hint.text: No prior knowledge needed — the steps take a few minutes. You can switch devices later by re-using your recovery phrase.
footer_hint.doc: Reassurance footer at the bottom of /welcome.html.

intro.text: Stoop is a research preview. The points below are never hidden — they apply to everyone who joins.

data_location_heading.doc: H3 above the data-location list on /privacy.html: which provider hosts what.

unset_value.doc: Placeholder shown next to a privacy-row label when no value is configured.

row_relay_operator.doc: Label for the relay operator row.

row_relay_url.doc: Label for the relay server URL row.

row_pod_issuer.doc: Label for the pod provider (Solid OIDC issuer) row.

row_pod_root.doc: Label for the pod root URL row.

claims_heading.doc: Section heading above the per-item claim list on MineScreen (mobile).

no_active_group.doc: Empty state on MineScreen when no bundle is active.

intro.text: Stoop works fully locally out of the box. A Solid pod is optional: it makes your posts and profile portable across devices and gives you control over storage. You can sign in or out later without losing data.
intro.doc: Lead paragraph. Contains <strong> tag — keep it.

issuer_label.doc: Label above the issuer URL input.

submit.doc: Submit button on the sign-in form.

sign_out.doc: Submit button on the sign-out form.

pod_attached.doc: Status word shown when the pod is currently attached.

pod_not_attached.doc: Status word shown when sign-in succeeded but pod-attach failed.

signed_in_html.doc: Status line shown when signed in. Contains <strong> + two placeholders.

not_signed_in.text: Not signed in to a pod. Stoop is running locally.
not_signed_in.doc: Status line shown when no pod is attached.

signed_out.doc: Confirmation shown after a successful sign-out.

intro.text: Counters of actions this agent has seen in this process. In-memory — reset on restart. Supports the beta runbook § "Operational metrics".

refresh.doc: Refresh button on /metrics.html — triggers an immediate reload of counters.

empty.text: No counters yet — nothing has happened in this session.
empty.doc: Empty-state shown when the metrics map is empty.

col_event.doc: Header of the first column in the metrics grid.

col_count.doc: Header of the second column — number of times the event fired.

col_last.doc: Header of the third column — timestamp of the most recent firing.

snapshot_at.doc: Footer line under the metrics grid. {ts} is a locale-formatted timestamp.

errors.name_required: Please give the group a name.
errors.itemId_required: Pick a post to report first.

post_form.hide_name_hint: Other members see your handle (@…), not your real name. Not anonymous against the server — the relay operator can still see that you posted.

pictures_label.doc: Label of the picker on the post-form. Phase 39 — V2.5 picture attachments.

pictures_hint.text: Up to 4 pictures; resized locally (max 1280px) before posting. Other members see a thumbnail; the full image is loaded on click.
pictures_hint.doc: Hint under the picker. Explains the 4-picture cap, the client-side resize, and the on-request loading.

pictures_resizing.doc: Inline status while images are being resized in the browser.

pictures_ready.doc: Inline status after a successful resize. {count} is an integer.

pictures_error.doc: Inline status when one or more images failed to resize. {count} is an integer.

open_picture.doc: ARIA label on a thumbnail in the feed. Triggers the modal that loads the full image.

close.doc: Close button label on the attachment modal.

picture_loading.doc: Modal status text shown while the full bytes are being fetched.

picture_pending.text: Loading picture — may take a moment if the sender is offline.
picture_pending.doc: Modal status text shown when the requestAttachment skill returned `pending: true`. The fetch continues in the background.

attach_picture.doc: Tooltip on the camera icon next to the chat input. Phase 39 — V2.5 picture attachments. Used as the title attribute on the picker label.

permission_camera_rationale.text: Stoop wants to use the camera to scan QR codes and add photos to posts.
permission_camera_rationale.doc: Permission rationale string shown when first asking for camera access on Android. Used by expo-camera + expo-image-picker. Stoop V3 Phase 40.5/40.6.

permission_gallery_rationale.text: Stoop wants access to your photos so you can add one to a post.
permission_gallery_rationale.doc: Permission rationale shown when first asking for media-library access on Android.

permission_location_rationale.text: Stoop wants to read your location to show distance-filtered posts.
permission_location_rationale.doc: Permission rationale shown when first asking for location access.

permission_push_rationale.text: Stoop wants to notify you when someone replies to your post or sends a new message.
permission_push_rationale.doc: Permission rationale shown right before the OS push prompt. Phase 40.9.

take_photo.doc: Camera-CTA on the post-form / chat input.

pick_from_library.doc: Library-CTA next to the camera-CTA on the post-form.

scan_qr.doc: Primary CTA on the welcome / onboard screen — opens the camera to a barcode scanner.

scan_recognised_invite.doc: Inline status when an invite QR is decoded.

scan_recognised_contact.doc: Inline status when a stoop-contact:// QR is decoded.

scan_recognised_recovery.doc: Inline status when a 12/24-word recovery phrase QR is decoded.

scan_unrecognised.doc: Inline status when a scanned QR doesn't match any known Stoop payload.

brand.doc: Brand wordmark on the welcome screen. Stoop V3 Phase 40.10.

tagline.text: Neighbourhood skill-app — ask your neighbours for help.
tagline.doc: Sub-headline under the brand on the welcome screen.

cta_new.doc: Primary CTA on the welcome screen — generates a fresh local identity. (Mobile fresh-installs default to local-only; pod sign-in is opt-in later.)

cta_scan.doc: Secondary CTA on the welcome screen — opens the camera scanner (OnboardScanScreen).

privacy_link.doc: Footer link on the welcome screen.

subheading.text: Point the camera at a Stoop QR. You can also paste it.

grant_camera.doc: Button shown when the OS hasn't granted camera permission yet.

paste_link.doc: Link below the camera surface that reveals the paste box.

paste_placeholder.text: Paste a Stoop invite, contact link, or recovery phrase

paste_submit.doc: Submit button for the paste-fallback textarea.

subheading.text: Enter your recovery phrase (12 or 24 words, separated by spaces).

submitting.doc: Submit button label while the restore call is in flight.

status_too_short.doc: Inline status when the user has typed only a few words.

status_wrong_count.text: A recovery phrase is 12, 15, 18, 21 or 24 words.
status_wrong_count.doc: Inline status when the word count is wrong.

status_malformed_word.text: A word looks off — only lowercase letters please.
status_malformed_word.doc: Inline status when one of the words isn't BIP-39-shaped.

status_looks_ok.doc: Inline status when the structural check passes.

empty_no_items.text: No posts in this neighbourhood yet.
empty_no_items.doc: Empty state when the feed has no items at all.

empty_filtered.doc: Empty state when items exist but the active filter excludes them all.

kind_vraag.doc: Filter chip label for kind=vraag (asks/help-needed posts).

kind_aanbod.doc: Filter chip label for kind=aanbod (offer/help-available posts).

no_group.text: Join a group first to see the pinboard.
no_group.doc: Empty-state on mobile FeedScreen when no agent is active (no group joined yet). Stoop V3 mobile.

mine.doc: Bottom-tab label for the MineScreen (own posts).

kind_vraag.doc: Kind-toggle chip label for kind=vraag on PostComposeScreen.

kind_aanbod.doc: Kind-toggle chip label for kind=aanbod on PostComposeScreen.

distance_label.doc: Section heading above the distance ChipRow on PostComposeScreen.

distance_km.doc: Distance preset format — {n} is one of 1/2/5/10/25.

audience_label.text: Who do you want to post to?
audience_label.doc: Section heading above the AudiencePicker on PostComposeScreen.

audience_hint_default.doc: Hint when no audience is explicitly selected.

audience_hint_n.doc: Hint when one or more targets are selected. {n} is the count.

no_group.text: Join a group first so you can post.
no_group.doc: Empty-state on mobile PostComposeScreen when no agent is active.

skills_label.doc: Section label above the skill chips.

submitting.doc: Submit CTA label while the call is in flight.

too_many_attachments.doc: Inline alert when the user exceeds MAX_ATTACHMENTS.

permission_denied.doc: Inline error when the OS rejected the permission prompt.

section_local.text: Stoop runs on your device — there's no central server in the middle.

section_pod.text: If you connect a Solid pod, you control your own data and who can read it.

section_handles.text: Others see your @handle by default. You can reveal a real name to specific people.

section_location.text: Your location is rounded to a 500m grid and only shared if you opt in.

section_no_thirdparty.text: No analytics, no ad-trackers, no third-party cloud providers.

body.text: Connect your Solid pod to sync your profile + posts across devices.

placeholder.text: Pod sign-in is not wired in this build. V3 ships local-only by default; pod connection lands in a follow-up build.
placeholder.doc: Placeholder body shown until bring-up code wires useSignInHook.

heading.text: Show this QR to your neighbour

body.text: Have the other device scan this QR from the Stoop welcome screen.

no_invite.text: No invite available. Open this screen via Group → Issue invite.
no_invite.doc: Empty state when route opened without an invite param.

unavailable.text: Stats are not wired in this build.
unavailable.doc: Empty state when bring-up code didn't pass a snapshot.

unknown.doc: Empty state on ContactScreen when the route was opened without a resolved contact.

open_chat.doc: Primary CTA on ContactScreen — navigates to a 1:1 thread.

show_qr.doc: CTA — reveals the user's stoop-contact:// share QR.

qr_caption.text: Show this to your contact so you can mark each other as known.

mute.doc: Mute action on ContactScreen — silences notifications.

confirm_mute_body.text: You won't get notifications from this contact.

confirm_block_body.text: Blocked contacts can no longer message you.

placeholder.doc: Placeholder of the message composer in ChatThreadScreen.

empty.doc: Empty state inside an open thread.

request_reveal.doc: CTA in the thread header that opens the bilateral-reveal flow.

reveal_unavailable.text: Reveal flow not available in this build.
reveal_unavailable.doc: Alert shown when onRequestReveal is missing (placeholder-only build).

unknown_item.doc: Empty state on ItemDetailScreen when route was opened without a resolved item.

chat.doc: Primary CTA — opens / creates a 1:1 thread with the author.

claim.doc: Secondary CTA — bring-up code wires this to the claim flow.

hide.doc: Overflow CTA — soft-mutes the item for this user.

report.doc: Overflow CTA — surfaces the report flow.

respond.doc: Primary CTA on a non-self item — calls respondToItem skill.

responding.doc: Primary CTA label while the respond call is in flight.

cancel.doc: Destructive CTA visible to the post author — calls cancelRequest.

mark_returned.doc: Lend-only CTA visible to the post author — calls markReturned.

no_active_group.text: Join a group first to view posts.
no_active_group.doc: Empty-state when ItemDetailScreen is opened without an active bundle.

unknown_member.doc: Empty state on ProfileOtherScreen when the route was opened without a member to render.

holiday.doc: Heading shown when the member has holiday-mode on.

holiday_body.doc: Body text under the holiday heading.

location.doc: Section heading for the member's coarse location.

request_reveal.doc: CTA on ProfileOtherScreen — opens the bilateral-reveal flow.

open_chat.doc: CTA on ProfileOtherScreen — opens a 1:1 chat.

no_active_group.text: Join a group first to view profiles.
no_active_group.doc: Empty-state on ProfileOtherScreen when there's no active bundle.

contact_detail.doc: Secondary CTA on ProfileOtherScreen that opens the per-contact settings screen (Phase 40.18).

intro_html.text: Your handle is what others see by default — e.g. @oosterpoort-bird-23. Your real name is optional and only visible to those you reveal it to.
intro_html.doc: Lead paragraph explaining the handle vs. real-name model. Contains <strong> and <code> tags.

avatar_clear.doc: Avatar 'remove / clear' button next to the file picker.

avatar_pick_image.doc: Inline error shown when the user tried to upload a non-image as avatar.

avatar_resizing.doc: Inline status while the avatar is being resized in the browser to 256px.

avatar_saved_kb.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful avatar save. {size} is the post-resize size in kilobytes.

avatar_cleared.doc: Inline confirmation after clearing the avatar.

handle_save.doc: Submit button for saving the handle.

handle_saved.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful handle save.

display_name_save.doc: Submit button for saving the display name.

display_name_saved.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful display-name save.

default_render_heading.text: How others see you by default

default_render_others_see.doc: Browser-bridge form (single-brace placeholder) of the default-render line.

is_revealed.doc: Suffix appended when the user has revealed their real name to this group.

handle_default.doc: Suffix appended when the user has NOT revealed their real name.

no_group_configured.doc: Placeholder shown when the bundle has no active group.

no_group.text: Join a group first — then you can fill in your profile.
no_group.doc: Empty-state body on mobile ProfileMineScreen when the user hasn't joined a group yet (Stoop's agent is per-group). Stoop V3 mobile.

saving.doc: Generic in-flight label on mobile ProfileMineScreen submit buttons.

holiday_label.doc: Label next to the holiday-mode toggle on mobile ProfileMineScreen.

holiday_on.doc: Holiday-mode toggle label when the flag is set.

holiday_off.doc: Holiday-mode toggle label when the flag is unset.

location_heading.doc: H2 above the location section on mobile ProfileMineScreen.

location_unset.doc: Body text when the user has not set a location.

location_capture.doc: CTA on mobile ProfileMineScreen that triggers GPS via expo-location.

location_busy.doc: CTA label while expo-location is in flight.

location_clear.doc: Ghost button on mobile ProfileMineScreen that drops the user's location.

recovery_heading.doc: H2 above the recovery section on mobile ProfileMineScreen.

recovery_intro.text: Keep your recovery phrase somewhere safe. You'll need it to install Stoop on another device.
recovery_intro.doc: Body explanation under the recovery heading.

recovery_show.doc: CTA that reveals the user's mnemonic in a confirm modal.

recovery_close.doc: Confirm-modal label on the recovery-reveal modal.

recovery_unavailable.text: Recovery export not available in this build.
recovery_unavailable.doc: Inline error when the recovery skill is missing or unwired.

handle_empty.doc: Inline error on mobile ProfileMineScreen for empty handle.

handle_bad_chars.doc: Inline error for handle with disallowed characters.

me_initial.doc: Single-letter fallback shown in the avatar circle. The first letter is used.

skills_intro_html.text: Tick what you can help with — others see this on your profile and it determines which asks are routed to you. You can optionally add free-form tags per skill (e.g. bicycle, repair) and set the status to paused or archived.

skill_status_active.doc: Status label for an active skill.

skill_status_paused.doc: Status label for a paused skill.

skill_status_archived.doc: Status label for an archived skill.

skill_tags_placeholder.doc: Placeholder of the per-skill free-tags input.

skill_saving.doc: Inline status while a skill change is being persisted.

skill_saved.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful skill change.

skill_tags_saved.doc: Inline confirmation after the per-skill tags blur-save.

no_categories.doc: Empty-state shown when listSkillCategories returned an empty array.

holiday_on.doc: Toggle-button label when holiday-mode is currently OFF (clicking turns it on).

holiday_off.doc: Toggle-button label when holiday-mode is currently ON (clicking turns it off).

holiday_active_html.text: You're on holiday. New asks / offers won't auto-flow to you.
holiday_active_html.doc: Status text when holiday-mode is ON. Contains <strong>.

holiday_inactive.text: You're available for automatic skill matches.
holiday_inactive.doc: Status text when holiday-mode is OFF.

location_intro.text: A coarse-grid location (snapped to 500m) lets you limit posts to a radius around you, or share distance with trusted contacts. Without location Stoop still works, just without the distance filter.
location_intro.doc: Lead paragraph for the location section.

location_geocode_warn_html.text: ⚠️ Your query is sent to OpenStreetMap (Nominatim) to look up coordinates. Your name and WebID are not sent.
location_geocode_warn_html.doc: Privacy warning under the geocode form. Contains <em> tag.

location_clear.doc: Button to clear the user's saved location.

location_search.doc: Submit button for the geocode query.

location_confirm.doc: Confirm button on the geocode preview card.

location_cancel.doc: Cancel button on the geocode preview card.

location_searching.doc: Inline status while the geocode skill is running.

location_saved.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful setMyLocation.

location_none.doc: Status shown when no location is set on the user's profile.

location_current_html.doc: Status shown when a location IS set. Contains <strong> + <span class="hint"> tags.

location_preview_html.doc:
Body of the geocode preview card before confirmation. Contains ,
, tags.

recovery_intro_html.text: Your recovery phrase is the only way to get your account back on a new device. We show it once — write it down and keep it somewhere safe (don't screenshot).
recovery_intro_html.doc: Lead paragraph for the recovery section. Contains <strong> + <em> tags.

show_mnemonic.doc: Button to reveal the BIP-39 mnemonic phrase. Pressing it irreversibly marks the phrase as 'shown'.

mnemonic_already_shown.text: Recovery phrase already revealed — for safety it can't be shown again.
mnemonic_already_shown.doc: Status shown when the user has already revealed the mnemonic in this install.

mnemonic_write_now.text: Write these 12 words down now. They will not be shown again.
mnemonic_write_now.doc: Status shown right after the mnemonic is revealed.

backup_intro.text: Create a backup file that only you can open with your password. The password never leaves this device.
backup_intro.doc: Lead paragraph for the encrypted-backup form.

backup_download.doc: Submit button for the encrypted-backup form.

backup_encrypting.doc: Inline status while the encryptedBackup skill is running.

backup_downloaded.text: Backup downloaded. Keep the file and your password separately.
backup_downloaded.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful backup download.

redeem_hero_title.doc: Hero heading shown in redeem mode (?invite=…).

redeem_hero_intro.text: You've been invited to a neighbourhood group. Stoop runs on your own device — there is no central server. You can take your data with you later, or delete it.

gates_heading.doc: H2 above the privacy + house-rules accept gates.

gate_privacy_html.text: I agree with the privacy notice: my posts and chats stay within the group, and I can export or delete them later.
gate_privacy_html.doc: Label of the privacy-acceptance checkbox. Contains <strong>.

gate_rules_html.text: I have read the house rules of this group and agree with them.
gate_rules_html.doc: Label of the house-rules-acceptance checkbox. Contains <strong>.

no_rules_yet.text: This group hasn't published any house rules (yet).
no_rules_yet.doc: Status when the group has no house rules.

rules_fetch_failed.text: (Couldn't fetch the house rules — ask the admin to send them via chat.)

handle_heading.text: Choose your handle for this group
handle_heading.doc: H3 above the handle input in redeem mode.

handle_intro_html.text: Others see only your handle by default (e.g. @oosterpoort-vogel-12) — not your real name. You can do a mutual reveal per chat later.
handle_intro_html.doc: Hint above the handle input. Contains <code>.

redeem_submit.doc: Submit button on the redeem form.

after_join_label.doc: Lead-in for the post-join save-recovery-phrase link.

save_phrase_link.doc: Link text pointing at /profile.html after join.

invite_unreadable.doc: Error shown when ?invite=… can't be decoded.

gate_required.doc: Error shown when one of the privacy/rules checkboxes is unchecked at submit.

joining.doc: Status while redeemInvite is in flight.

refused.doc: Prefix shown before the gate-skill's error code.

joined_as.doc: Success status after a successful join.

issue_heading.doc: H2 in issue mode (admin generating an invite link).

issue_intro.text: Generate a link for one new member. Send it out-of-band: chat app, mail, paper note. The link is single-use and expires after the TTL.

ttl_label.doc: Label of the TTL number input.

invite_result_heading.text: Send this link to the new member
invite_result_heading.doc: H3 above the generated invite link.

copied.doc: Temporary button label after a successful copy.

no_invite_admin_only.text: No invitation received — are you admin of this group?
no_invite_admin_only.doc: Error shown when issueInvite returned no invite.

intro_html.text: Contacts live alongside groups. With trusted contacts you can share extra things (location, posts outside groups). Acquainted contacts only see what you explicitly share.
intro_html.doc: Lead paragraph explaining contact vs. group separation + the trust levels. Contains <em>.

empty.text: No contacts yet. Add one via QR or manually.

request_wants_to_add_html.doc: Body of an incoming-contact-request card. Contains <em>.

accept.doc: Accept button on an incoming contact request.

decline.doc: Decline / ignore button on an incoming contact request.

flag_share_location.doc: Per-contact toggle: share my location with this contact.

flag_hop_through.doc: Per-contact toggle: let this contact relay messages through my device.

flag_auto_match.doc: Per-contact toggle: accept auto-skill-match suggestions from this contact.

flag_on.doc: Lower-case 'on' suffix for per-contact flag buttons.

flag_off.doc: Lower-case 'off' suffix for per-contact flag buttons.

tags_placeholder.doc: Placeholder for the per-contact tags input.

remove_confirm.doc: JS confirm() dialog before removing a contact.

qr_heading.doc: H3 for the QR / paste-link sub-flow.

qr_intro_html.text: Show someone your QR (they scan it in their Stoop), or paste their stoop-contact:// link here.
qr_intro_html.doc: Hint under the QR heading. Contains <code>.

qr_paste_label_html.doc: Label of the paste-link input. Contains <code>.

add_button.doc: Submit button — reused on multiple add-contact forms.

added.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful add.

added_named.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful QR-add.

contact_fallback.doc: Fallback word when the added contact has neither handle nor webid yet.

qr_card_intro.text: Send this link / show the QR:
qr_card_intro.doc: Lead text above the user's own share-QR payload.

qr_card_footer.text: Valid until you regenerate it. You can copy the link or run it through a QR renderer.
qr_card_footer.doc: Hint under the user's own share-QR payload.

manual_webid_label.doc: Label of the manual WebID input.

manual_pubkey_label.doc: Label of the manual pubKey input.

manual_pubkey_placeholder.text: optional — without it we can only reach them after a first hello

lists_intro_html.text: Your own grouping of contacts — e.g. school parents or family. Lists are local to you; the contacts themselves don't see them.
lists_intro_html.doc: Lead paragraph for the contact-lists section. Contains <em>.

new_list_button.doc: Submit button for creating a new contact list.

rename.doc: Rename button on a contact list.

delete.doc: Delete button on a contact list.

list_rename_prompt.doc: JS prompt() for renaming a contact list.

list_delete_confirm.doc: JS confirm() before deleting a contact list.

list_empty_inline.doc: Inline placeholder when a contact list has no members.

contact_count_one.doc: Singular form of the per-list contact-count chip.

contact_count_many.doc: Plural form of the per-list contact-count chip.

search_placeholder.doc: Placeholder of the mobile ContactsScreen search box. Stoop V3 mobile.

muted.doc: Inline tag next to a contact's name when muted (mobile).

empty_no_contacts.doc: Empty state on mobile ContactsScreen when the list is empty.

empty_filtered.doc: Empty state on mobile ContactsScreen when the search filter excludes everything.

intro.text: Get notified on this device when something happens — even when Stoop is in the background. Runs through your browser; you can revoke permission anytime in browser settings.

subscribe.doc: Button label to enable push notifications.

unsubscribe.doc: Button label to disable push notifications.

test.doc: Button label that fires a self-test push.

unsupported.text: This browser does not support Web Push.
unsupported.doc: Status line shown when Service Worker / PushManager is missing.

no_vapid.text: Push is disabled on this server — ask the admin to configure a VAPID key.
no_vapid.doc: Status line shown when the relay operator hasn't configured a VAPID key.

status_subscribed_html.doc: Status line shown when a push subscription exists. Contains <code> + {perm} placeholder.

status_not_subscribed_html.text: Not yet subscribed to push notifications (perm: {perm}).
status_not_subscribed_html.doc: Status line shown when no push subscription exists. Contains <code> + {perm} placeholder.

subscribed_ok.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful subscribe.

unsubscribed_ok.doc: Inline confirmation after a successful unsubscribe.

test_sending.doc: Inline status while a test push is in flight.

test_body.doc: Body of the test push notification.

test_result.doc: Inline status after the test push.

enable.doc: CTA on mobile PushScreen — kicks off requestPushPermission + setupPush. Stoop V3 mobile.

enabling.doc: CTA label while in flight on mobile PushScreen.

enabled.doc: Idle CTA label after enable succeeded on mobile PushScreen.

status_unknown.doc: Status panel on mobile PushScreen when push hasn't been turned on yet.

status_denied.text: Permission denied. Enable notifications via Settings.
status_denied.doc: Status panel on mobile when permission was denied.

status_granted_no_agent.text: Permission OK. Waiting for agent bring-up.
status_granted_no_agent.doc: Status panel when perm is granted but the agent isn't running yet on mobile.

status_no_token.doc: Status panel when expo didn't return a token on mobile.

status_enabled.doc: Success status panel on mobile PushScreen.

intro.text: Starting a group is not just a click. It is an agreement between members. Answer the six questions below; they become the group's rules, visible to everyone who joins later.
intro.doc: Lead paragraph on /create-group.html. Frames group creation as a social agreement. Contains <strong> tag.

q1_name.doc: Field label for the group's display name.

q1_purpose.doc: Field label for the two-sentence purpose statement.

q1_purpose_ph.text: e.g. Neighbours in Groningen-Oosterpoort who want to help each other with chores, lending, and staying in the loop.

q2_admins.text: 2. Who is admin? (WebIDs, one per line)
q2_admins.doc: Field label for the admins-list textarea.

q2_admins_hint.text: We recommend at least two admins.
q2_admins_hint.doc: Hint under the admins textarea recommending two admins.

q3_rules.text: 3. House rules — what fits / does not fit (one per line)
q3_rules.doc: Field label for the house-rules textarea.

q3_rules_ph.text:
Asks, offers, lending.
Default language: English.
No ads, no politics.
q3_rules_ph.doc: Placeholder for the house-rules textarea, multi-line example.

q4_conflict.doc: Field label for the conflict-resolution policy select.

q4_conflict_mediation.text: Conversation between parties, admin as witness

q5_access.doc: Field label for the access-policy select.

q5_invite_only.text: By invitation — admin hands out the QR personally

q5_shared_link.text: Via shared link — members can add others

q6_leave.doc: Field label for the leave-policy select.

q6_keep_posts.text: Posts stay, name disappears (default — preserves context)

q6_remove_all.text: Everything from that person disappears (clean break)

group_id_label.doc: Field label for the technical group-id input.

rotation_intro.text: The group uses a new code every X days. Anyone who no longer receives the code automatically falls out of the group. You exchange the code outside the app — e.g. via WhatsApp, on a slip of paper, or in person. That keeps the group local.
rotation_intro.doc: Hint under the rotation legend. Contains <em> + <strong> tags AND deliberately mentions the in-person / out-of-band channel.

rotation_admin_only_label.doc: Bold label for the admin-only rotation radio.

rotation_admin_only_hint.text: Admins generate a fresh code every X days and hand it to members personally.
rotation_admin_only_hint.doc: Hint under the admin-only rotation radio.

rotation_peer_label.text: Anyone may pass on the code.
rotation_peer_label.doc: Bold label for the peer-distributable rotation radio.

rotation_peer_hint.text: Any member can give the code to a newcomer. More decentralised — admin is not a single point of failure.
rotation_peer_hint.doc: Hint under the peer-distributable rotation radio.

rotation_days_label.doc: Label above the rotation-period number input.

saving.doc: Inline status while createGroupV2 is running.

created_msg.doc: Inline success status. {groupId} is the new group's id.

result_intro.text: The first membership code is below — give it outside Stoop to the first members:
result_intro.doc: Lead text above the first membership code. Reinforces out-of-band distribution.

result_expiry.doc: Expiry line under the membership code.

result_footer.text: You can view it again later on the group page.
result_footer.doc: Footer hint under the result card.

mode_admin_only.doc: Lower-case description of admin-only rotation, plugged into result_expiry's {mode}.

mode_peer.text: anyone may pass on the code
mode_peer.doc: Lower-case description of peer-distributable rotation, plugged into result_expiry's {mode}.

feed.doc: Top-nav link to the main feed page (/).

my_posts.doc: Top-nav link to /mine.html — the user's own posts.

chat.doc: Top-nav link to /chat.html — peer-to-peer chat threads.

contacts.doc: Top-nav link to /contacts.html — the user's contact book.

metrics.doc: Top-nav link to /metrics.html — diagnostic metrics page.

profile.doc: Top-nav link to /profile.html — user's own profile + handle.

pod.doc: Top-nav link to /sign-in.html — Solid pod sign-in / status.

push.doc: Top-nav link to /push.html — Web-Push subscription management.

onboard.doc: Top-nav link to /onboard.html — admin invite / onboarding tools.

eviction_banner_title.doc: Heading of the orange banner on /group.html shown when one or more members' codes have expired past the grace window.

eviction_banner_intro.text: One or more members' membership code has expired — their posts are hidden until they redeem the new code.
eviction_banner_intro.doc: Lead paragraph in the eviction banner. Explains the auto-evict behaviour from Phase 35 (V2.5).

eviction_member_html.doc: Single list item inside the eviction banner. Contains a <strong> tag and two placeholders: {webid} (URL) and {date} (locale-formatted timestamp). Preserve both.

unnamed.doc: Heading fallback when group.name is missing on mobile GroupScreen.

evicted_title.text: You have been removed from this group.
evicted_title.doc: Eviction banner title on mobile GroupScreen.

evicted_body.text: You can no longer see posts or messages in this group. Ask the admin to invite you again.
evicted_body.doc: Eviction banner body on mobile GroupScreen.

member_count.doc: Label for the member-count row on mobile GroupScreen.

admin_code.doc: Label for the admin-code row on mobile GroupScreen (admin only).

admin_code_hint.text: Only share this code with other admins.
admin_code_hint.doc: Hint under the admin code on mobile GroupScreen.

issue_invite.doc: Admin CTA on mobile GroupScreen — generates a fresh invite token + opens OnboardIssue.

issuing.doc: CTA label while issuing on mobile.

confirm_leave_body.text: You will lose access to all posts and messages in this group.

confirm_leave_yes.doc: Destructive confirm label on mobile GroupScreen.

page_title.doc: Browser tab title for /auth-callback.html, the OIDC redirect target page.

busy.doc: Initial status line shown while completePodSignIn is running, before bulk-sync starts.

uploading.text: Uploading your local data to your pod…
uploading.doc: Status line shown when bulk-sync transitions to 'running' (Phase 34, V2.5).

progress.doc: Progress label under the bar. {done} and {total} are integer counts of bulk-sync paths; do not localise the placeholders.

signed_in_html.text: Signed in as {webid}. Pod attached at {podRoot}.
signed_in_html.doc: Success line after sign-in. Contains <strong> + <code> tags AND two placeholders: {webid} (URL) and {podRoot} (URL); preserve both.

synced_summary.doc: Single-line summary shown after a clean bulk-sync finish. {count} is an integer.

sync_partial.text: Sign-in succeeded; some local data could not upload yet — Stoop will retry in the background.
sync_partial.doc: Warning line shown when bulk-sync ends with phase 'error'.

back_link.doc: Bottom-of-page link back to /sign-in.html. Trailing arrow is intentional.

intro.text: Stoop keeps your settings in two places: things that belong to this device (like how often your phone refreshes) stay local; my preferences follow you across all your devices via your pod.
intro.doc: Lead paragraph explaining the device/shared split. Contains <em> tags — keep them so the emphasis is preserved.

section_device_title.doc: Section heading for device-scoped settings (V2.5 Phase 33). Stays local to this install.

badge_device.doc: Small badge next to the device-section heading. Single word; should signal 'this device only'.

section_device_hint.text: These settings belong to this device. They can differ between your phone and your laptop.
section_device_hint.doc: Subtitle under the device-section heading. Reassures the user the values can differ across their installs.

section_shared_title.doc: Section heading for shared / pod-portable settings (V2.5 Phase 33). Travels with the user.

badge_shared.doc: Small badge next to the shared-section heading. Should signal 'syncs across devices'.

section_shared_hint.text: These preferences belong to you — not to this device. When you sign in to your pod, they automatically follow you to your other devices.
section_shared_hint.doc: Subtitle under the shared-section heading. Tells the user pod-sign-in is what enables cross-device sync.

poll_interval_label.doc: Label for the polling-interval select. Device-scoped.

poll_interval_hint.text: Lower = see new posts faster; higher = lighter on data and battery.
poll_interval_hint.doc: Hint under the polling-interval label. Trade-off summary: faster vs. battery.

poll_2s.doc: Polling option: 2 seconds. The '(live)' annotation marks it as the always-on default.

hop_label.doc: Label for the global hop-relay toggle. Device-scoped because it's a per-machine routing decision.

hop_hint.text: May my device relay messages on behalf of others? Default off: nobody passes through you. Per-contact setting lives on the contacts page.
hop_hint.doc: Hint under the hop-relay toggle. Default-off explanation + cross-link to /contacts.html. Contains <em> tags.

toggle_on.doc: Generic 'on' label next to a toggle. Reused across multiple toggles on /settings.html.

online_window_heading.doc: Sub-heading inside the device section for the mobile-only online-window controls.

online_window_hint.text: On phone (V3): how often and how long can my agent connect to the relay? Ignored on desktop — there the agent is always online.
online_window_hint.doc: Hint paragraph under the online-window heading. Mobile-only feature description.

online_every_label.doc: Label for the online-window 'every X minutes' field. Device-scoped.

online_every_hint.text: Connect once every X minutes. Empty = always connected (V2 desktop default).
online_every_hint.doc: Hint under the window-frequency input. Explains empty-means-always.

online_duration_label.doc: Label for the online-window 'duration in seconds' field. Device-scoped.

online_duration_hint.text: Number of seconds online per window.

online_empty_placeholder.doc: Placeholder text in the online-window number inputs when no value is set. Should signal 'optional / unset'.

broadcastable_label.doc: Label for the broadcastable toggle. Shared-scoped.

broadcastable_hint.text: May an unknown contact send me a skill-match suggestion that my agent filters? You only get notified on a match; no match = silence.
broadcastable_hint.doc: Hint under the auto-skill-match toggle. Explains the filtering + match-only-notification semantics.

default_share_location_label.text: Share location with new contacts by default?
default_share_location_label.doc: Label for the default-share-location toggle. Shared-scoped.

default_share_location_hint.text: Decides whether shareLocation defaults to on when you add someone. You can always override per-contact later.
default_share_location_hint.doc: Hint under the default-share-location toggle. Contains <code> tag for the field name.

feedback_saving.doc: Inline feedback text shown while a settings save is in flight.

feedback_saved.doc: Inline feedback text shown after a successful settings save.

shared_heading.doc: Mobile section heading for the cross-device portion of settings (Stoop V3 mobile).

device_heading.doc: Mobile section heading for the per-device portion (Stoop V3 mobile).

privacy_heading.doc: Mobile section heading on SettingsScreen (Stoop V3 mobile).

handle.doc: Label for the user handle row on mobile SettingsScreen.

display_name.doc: Label for the displayName row on mobile SettingsScreen.

location.doc: Label for the location row on mobile SettingsScreen.

location_unset.doc: Empty marker when no location is set on mobile SettingsScreen.

edit_profile.doc: CTA on mobile SettingsScreen — navigates to ProfileMine.

poll_interval.doc: Label for the pollIntervalMs input on mobile SettingsScreen.

poll_hint.doc: Inline hint under the poll-interval input on mobile.

saving.doc: Submit button label while in flight on mobile.

push_link.doc: Link to PushScreen from mobile SettingsScreen.

privacy_link.doc: Link to PrivacyScreen from mobile SettingsScreen.

page_title.doc: Browser tab title + main page heading on /restore.html — the recovery-from-mnemonic flow.

intro.text: Have your recovery phrase (12 or 24 words) from another device? Enter it below. Stoop will then sign in to your pod and pull all your posts, contacts and settings.
intro.doc: Lead paragraph on /restore.html. Sets expectations: (1) you need a recovery phrase from another device, (2) the app will pull state from your pod after restoring.

step1_heading.doc: Heading of step 1 of /restore.html: the user types their 12-or-24-word BIP-39 phrase.

step1_hint.text: The phrase never leaves this device. We check first that it's valid.
step1_hint.doc: Reassurance subtext under step 1: phrase is processed locally, validated before any state change.

step1_placeholder.doc: Placeholder inside the textarea where the user types the recovery phrase. Does NOT need to be a real BIP-39 phrase.

step1_button.doc: Submit button on step 1 — runs validateMnemonicPhrase skill.

step1_status_busy.doc: Inline status text shown while the validation skill is running.

step1_status_ok.doc: Inline status text shown after successful mnemonic validation; advances to step 2.

step2_heading.doc: Heading of step 2 of /restore.html: user reviews the derived pubKey and confirms the destructive swap.

step2_body.text: This would become your public key:
step2_body.doc: Lead text in step 2 above the pubKey preview block.

step2_warning.text: ⚠️ Confirming overwrites the current identity on this device. Posts / contacts / settings stored locally will be replaced when Stoop restarts.
step2_warning.doc: Warning box in step 2 — explains the destructive nature of restoring. The 'when Stoop restarts' hint is legacy V2 wording (Phase 31 made this immediate); revisit when re-translating.

step2_confirm.doc: Primary action button in step 2 — triggers restoreFromMnemonic skill with confirm:true.

step2_cancel.doc: Secondary button in step 2 — cancels the restore + returns to step 1.

step2_status_busy.doc: Inline status text while the restore skill is running.

step2_confirm_prompt.text: Are you sure? The current identity on this device will be overwritten.
step2_confirm_prompt.doc: Browser confirm() prompt shown when the user clicks Confirm. Last chance to back out.

step3_heading.doc: Heading of step 3 of /restore.html: success screen. Tells the user the swap completed and they're under the recovered identity.

step3_body.text: The restored identity is active right now. No restart needed.
step3_body.doc: Lead text in step 3. Phase 31 removed the manual restart step.

step3_hint.text: Want to bring your old posts, contacts and settings back to this device? Sign in to your pod — Stoop pulls everything automatically. Otherwise, just keep going.
step3_hint.doc: Hint paragraph in step 3 explaining the optional pod-sign-in for full state restore.

step3_pod_button.doc: Button in step 3 linking to /sign-in.html for the optional pod sync.

step3_home_button.doc: Secondary button in step 3 linking to / (the buurt-prikbord) for users who don't need pod sync.

nav_back.doc: Back link in the page header — returns to /welcome.html.
